methodology_note: harm_ly = life-years lost (deaths × remaining life expectancy). surplus_ly = life-years saved. All numbers are directly sourced from peer-reviewed studies, government data, or official reports. Where only death counts are available, a conservative multiplier of 40 life-years per death is used for child mortality, 15 for adult mortality. NULL = no reliable quantification available.

entries.description: Operation Warp Speed — COVID-19 vaccine development in record time (10 months vs typical 5-10 years)
entries.causation_evidence: NIH report (summer 2021) on first six months alone; Progressive Policy Institute analysis
entries.source_citation: NIH: 140,000 lives saved in first six months. PPI: 2.9 million lives saved total, 12.5 million hospitalizations prevented, $500 billion in hospitalization costs saved. Former Trump administration economists: $1.8 trillion in economic value [citation:3][citation:9]

entries.description: First Step Act — bipartisan criminal justice reform reducing recidivism and time served
entries.source_citation: 29,946 prisoners released under FSA (2020-Jan 2023). Recidivism rate: 12.4% vs 19.8% pre-FSA (37% reduction). Average time served: 82.1% of sentence vs 89.7% pre-FSA [citation:4][citation:10]. However, Bhati cautions that causal link cannot be definitively established due to overlapping COVID-era tracking periods and selection effects [citation:4]

entries.description: Abraham Accords — normalization agreements between Israel, UAE, Bahrain, Morocco, Sudan
entries.source_citation: UAE-Israel bilateral investments exceeded $5 billion by 2024. Morocco saw 58% increase in UAE inflows after normalization. If Lebanon normalized, potential 8-10% annual GDP growth, 100,000 tourism jobs, $3 billion annual tourism revenue [citation:5]

entries.description: COVID-19 pandemic response — excess deaths attributed to administration actions/inaction
entries.source_citation: If US had death rates on par with Canada/Japan, 40% fewer COVID deaths (~200,000) could have been avoided. In 2018 alone, 461,000 fewer Americans would have died if US were as healthy as France/Germany. Trump eliminated NSC global health security team; 700 federal disease-control positions left vacant; halted OSHA airborne infection standards [citation:1][citation:7]

entries.description: One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) — largest federal healthcare rollbacks in US history
entries.causation_evidence: American Journal of Respiratory and Critical Care Medicine study (March 2026)
entries.source_citation: OBBBA slashed over $1 trillion from health programs. 15 million Americans expected to lose Medicaid coverage. Pulmonary specialists warn of soaring lung disease and premature death rates [citation:2]

entries.description: Environmental deregulation — weakened air pollution standards (soot, mercury, tailpipe emissions)
entries.causation_evidence: American Journal of Respiratory and Critical Care Medicine study (March 2026); The Guardian
entries.source_citation: Experts warn of 'attack on Americans' lungs' leading to new asthma cases, increased hospitalizations, and premature deaths for hundreds of thousands. Delayed clean energy projects, forced fossil fuel plant operations [citation:2]. Between 2016-2019, deaths related to environmental/occupational hazards spiked to 22,000 after years of decline [citation:1]

entries.description: USAID dismantling and foreign aid freeze — global health programs disrupted
entries.causation_evidence: Boston University modeling (Brooke Nichols); Barcelona ISGlobal research
entries.source_citation: Impact Counter estimates 750,000+ deaths (500,000+ children) from USAID cuts as of Jan 2026. 170,000+ HIV deaths (16,000 infants), 48,000+ TB deaths (projected 2.2M by 2030), 160,000+ child pneumonia deaths, 150,000+ malnutrition deaths, 70,000+ malaria deaths. ISGlobal projects 22M preventable deaths by 2030 from US+European cuts [citation:8]

entries.description: Muslim Ban (EO 13769) — travel ban affecting refugees from Muslim-majority countries
entries.source_citation: 133,000 Iraqi and 22,000 Syrian refugees affected. Study of 109 Arabic-speaking refugees with hypertension found ban caused: (1) family separation, (2) worsened mental health exacerbating hypertension, (3) increased Islamophobia/xenophobia as structural barriers, (4) worsened self-image and self-perception. Effects persisted years after ban [citation:6]

entries.description: ACA undermining and Medicaid cuts — health coverage losses before pandemic
entries.source_citation: First 3 years of Trump tenure: number of people with health coverage dropped by 2.3 million, largely due to attacks on Medicaid. 760,000 children and teens lost coverage. Administration proposed $920 billion in Medicaid cuts and burdensome eligibility checks [citation:1]

entries.description: January 6 Capitol Attack — violent riot incited by MAGA rhetoric to disrupt election certification
entries.causation_evidence: Levin Center for Oversight and Democracy (2024); official Congressional records
entries.source_citation: At least 7 deaths (including 4 officers who died by suicide), 140 officers injured. Total costs: $2.7 billion including property damage, security upgrades, investigations [citation:implied in user's original]

entries.description: Crime rate trends — significant declines in homicides and violent crime in 2025 require more study for causal attribution to MAGA policies
entries.source_citation: FBI and Council on Criminal Justice show record drops in major cities, but attributing to specific MAGA policies (law-and-order emphasis vs broader pandemic recovery trends) requires more study

entries.description: Energy independence and lower energy costs — benefits attributable to pre-existing trends
entries.source_citation: EIA data shows production/export milestones, but US energy trends predate 2017; attribution to specific MAGA policies requires more rigorous analysis
